title: Fiery Fried Japanese Chicken Gyoza
description: A crowd-pleasing Japanese dish featuring crispy fried gyoza filled with spicy chicken and melted cheese, served in a terracotta dish.

ingredients.name: dried red chillies
ingredients.notes: crushed or 1 teaspoon dried red chilli flakes

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the crushed dried red chillies to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground chicken to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until it is no longer pink.
Stir in the soy sauce, salt, and black pepper, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Lay a gyoza wrapper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the chicken mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Top the chicken with a sprinkle of shredded mozzarella cheese.
Brush the edges of the gyoza wrapper with a small amount of water, then fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point.
Repeat with the remaining gyoza wrappers and filling.
Fry the gyoza in the skillet until they are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Transfer the fried gyoza to a terracotta dish and bake in the preheated oven for 5 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
